Handling Charge Setup

1.0 Overview  
Handling charges are calculated based on individual Active Principals, currencies, various charge types, and mode of calculation types. These variables are applied on the CBM to generate the actual Charges used in the Manifest Job File module.

2.0 Active Principals  
These are different foreign companies that are involved in freight forwarding, warehousing & distribution servives, ocean freight, air freight, trucking services, supply chain consulting etc. Their cargo handling charges are different based on their client cargo types. It is important to first register their names in DES before proceeding to apply how their charges are being applied.

*2.1 Where to setup Active Principals?*  
Active Principals are setup at the Cargo Manifest section when entering new cargo manifest details.

![][image1]

Fig 1.0: Cargo manifest screen

By clicking on the active principal setup button, the setup form comes up as seen in Fig 2.0 below  
![][image2]  
Fig 2.0: Principal setup screen

The screen allows user to input the principal name and by clicking on save, it stores in DB for later.

*2.2 Setup Currency*  
The next thing to setup is the currency and its rates to be used in the calculation.  
The currency setup is found under the Configuration\\Banking\\Currency setup as seen in Fig 3.0.

![][image3]

Fig 3.0: Currency setup menu

![][image4]  
Fig4.0: Currency Setup screen

The currency setup screen receives inputs for currency code, name of currency, rate, period within which it is operating and operational status to check for its being active or inactive. This part is important since it will be used in the handling charge setup.

*2.3 Handling Charge Setup for Active Principals*  
After setting up active Principal and currency rate, it is now time to set up handling charges for the active principal. Goto Manifest and click on the Handling Charge setup sub menu as seen fig 5a.

![][image5]  
Fig. 5a: Navigation to the Handling Charge Setup.

This action brings up the handling charge setup form as seen in the fig 5b below:

![][image6]  
Fig 5b: Handling charge setup for active principal.

A user is expected to double click on the active principal to begin the configuration. The name of the active principal seen on top of the form. The process is as follows:

1. Currency: Select the currency to use in the calculation.  
2. Mode of Calculation: Select the mode of calculation to apply. i.e. Min/Max, Max, Fixed.  
   1. Min/Max: Allows user to input the minimum and maximum values as seen on the form for a particular charge type.  
   2. Max: Allows user to input only the maximum value for a particular charge type.  
   3. Fixed: Allows user to enter only the fixed value for a particular charge type.  
3. Charge type: Allows user to enter the charge type and this is also based on the cargo type

*2.4 Calculation of Handling Charge* 

![][image7]  
Fig 6.0: Handling charge calculation under House BL.

The calculation of the handling charge is done under the house BLs tab of the cargo manifest module. It must be noted that, before clicking on the calculator icon two things must be ensured and they are:

1. The currency rate is active as described in section 2.2.  
2. The principal is active and has its handling charge setup as described in section 2.3.  
3. The House BL has a CBM value.

NB: GLC OCEAN LINE is being used as an example for the calculation of the handling charge.

By clicking on the Calculator icon (Fig 6.0), the follow action takes place:  
2.4.1 Total number (N) of charge types (arrays) for each principal is set to ten(10). This is assumed that, a principal cannot have more than ten charge types (Reference: Fig 5b )

2.4.2 Mode of calculation (Assuming for 4 Charge types)  
Each mode of calculation is put in a “placeholder” called HandChargeSub(X). “X” represents each charge type and in this scenario, there are 4 charge types hence X has a max of 4 and cannot go beyond that count.   
Firstly, retrieve the handling charge setup for “GLC Ocean Line” (Ref.: Fig 5b) which are   
X1: CFS – Min(25), Max(110)  
X2: DRYAGE – Min(16), Max(35)  
X3: GCNET – Fixed(70)  
X4: THC – Min(12), Max(30)

NB: CBM for GLC Ocean Line is “0.797” – Ref: Fig 6.0.  
Currency: USD with rate=GHC14  
Second, set X=1 because we are starting with the first record and test to ensure that if X\>N, then end the calculation process. Remember to use the minimum and maximum values derived in section 2.3 (Fig 5b) or under section 2.4.2 – X1…..X4. The purpose of the minimum and maximum value is that, if the system multiples the minimum value by the CBM and the results is less than the maximum value, then the handling charge for that charge type must be the maximum value.

In this scenario we are looking at **GLC OCEAN LINE** with 4 charge types (i.e. CFS, DRYAGE, GCNET, THC or records X1,X2,X3,X4) listed above with their various mode of calculation values  
	2.4.2.1 If mode of calculation in the 1st record (in this case X1: CFS) is Min/Max, then do the following:  
		Is “Min value” x Tot. CBM \< “Max value”   
		i.e. 25\*0.797 \< 110 – This is true so we set HandChargeSub(1)=110  
		If CBM were to be 5 then  
		25\*5 \< 110 – This is false so we will set HandChargeSub(1)=25\*5=125  
	

| Rec. \#: X | Charge Type | Mode of cal. | Min | CBM | Min \* CBM | Max | Fixed | Min\*CBM \< Max | HandCharge Sub(X) |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| 1 | CFS | Min/Max | 25 | 0.797 | 19.925 | 110 | \- | True | 110 |
| 2 | DRYAGE | Min/Max | 16 | 0.797 | 12.752 | 35 |  | True | 35 |
| 3 | GCNET | Fixed | \- | 0.797 | \- | \- | 70 | \- | 70 |
| 4 | THC | Min/Max | 12 | 0.797 | 9.564 | 30 |  | True | 30 |
| **Handling Charge** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | **245** |
| **USD Rate** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | **14** |
| **Total Handling Charge**  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | **3430** |

Table 1.0: Summary of Handling charge calculation

![][image8]  
Fig 7.0: Charge types information being displayed on the side of the charge field.

After computing the handling charge, clicking on the charge field will display the handling charge information. This information will appear on the receipt print out after customer pays for the handling charge.

**2.5 Application of Handling Charge at Manifest Jobfile**  
Go to create new manifest and type in the HBL no to search. In this case, we are using the HBL No.: MME083028.

![][image9]  
Fig 8.0: New Manifest Job file form

In fig. 8.0, the HBL No has not used in the Manifest Job File module. Input the HBL No. in the “House BL” and press on the tab key to lose focus. This action populates the Manifest Jobfile form with initial information gotten from the Cargo Manifest module as seen in Fig 9.0.

![][image10]  
Fig 9.0: Inputting HBL No and clicking outside the BL fields to populate initial manifest info.  
![][image11]  
Fig 10.0: Recompute Handling Charge

By clicking on the “Recompute Handling Charge” under the “Task Manager”, the system first gives a prompt as seen in Fig 10.1 to notify the user that the system will use the already configured Principal charge setting to calculate the handling charge. Choosing “Yes” will populate the Handling charge field with handling charge amount and its associated amount fields as seen in Fig 11.0.

![][image12]  
Fig 10.1: Message prompt

![][image13]  
Fig 11.0: Handling charge field and other fields populated with required amount values.

Goto task manager and save. This prompt user to print receipt. Choose “Yes” and a receipt as seen in Fig 11.1 would be printed out for the customer.

![][image14]  
Fig. 11.1: Print out showing section of Handling Charge.
